title: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
author: Stephen Clark
body: It seems as though $5.6 billion wasn't enough. That was the news from the US Space Force on Friday, when military officials announced they were tripling the maximum value of one of the service's National Security Space Launch contracts to $17 billion. The expansion of the Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3 contract comes as the Pentagon signals rising demand for military satellite launches. The NSSL program is set up to allow Space Systems Command, which oversees the Space Force's launch program, to select from a pool of launch providers for individual missions to deliver the military's satellites to orbit. The NSSL program has two parts. Lane 1 covers the Space Force's more risk-tolerant missions, such as medium-lift launches with experimental payloads or rideshare missions carrying satellites for the Pentagon's surveillance or data relay constellations . Lane 2 includes higher-priority strategic missions, like the government's largest and most expensive spy satellites, or radiation-hardened communications satellites designed to survive a nuclear war. title: Get Borderlands 3, Risk of Rain 2 and 13 other great PC games for $15
body: Borderlands 3 is among the 15 games in the 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle. | 2K Games The aptly-named “2K Megahits 2026 Bundle” from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. It’s a smattering of the publisher 2K’s biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain 2, Borderlands 3, and XCOM: Enemy Unknown . The bundle will be available until August 7th, though the bundle page notes that keys for some games are already starting to run low. All of the games work on Windows, while some support MacOS and SteamOS. 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle Where to Buy: $465 $15 at Humble One of my personal favorite games, Risk of Rain 2 , is included in the bundle. In the third-person roguelike, you’ll face off against increasingly difficult waves of enemies, fighting to stay alive as long as you can and collecting power-ups and useful perks before extracting to the next zone to do it all again. It’s just challenging enough to make victory satisfying, and fun to play alone or with friends. You also get 2012’s XCOM: Enemy Unknown , a legendary turn-based strategy game that’s usually $15 on its own, to give you a sense of the bundle’s value. The bundle also notably includes three games from the Borderlands universe. Borderlands 3 Special Edition is a traditional looter-shooter FPS, Tiny Tina’s Wonderlands adds RPG elements like an overworld map and spellcasting, and Tales from the Borderlands is an interactive episodic visual novel from Telltale Games. title: The moments that defined the 2026 World Cup
body: Every four years, the World Cup reminds us why we fall in love with sports in the first place. The 2026 tournament was one of firsts. For the first time, three countries Canada, Mexico, and the United States shared hosting duties. For the first time, the field expanded from 32 to 48 teams, giving more nations a chance to compete on the sport's biggest stage and more federations the opportunity to showcase their depth. That expansion brought historic debuts for Cape Verde, Jordan, Cura ao, and Uzbekistan. Their first appearances delivered some of the tournament's most unforgettable moments. It also felt like a farewell tour. Cristiano Ronaldo. Lionel Messi. Luka Modrić. Neymar. Son Heung-min. Guillermo Ochoa. A generation that shaped the sport for two decades took one last bow on the biggest stage, with several likely making their final World Cup appearances. Taken together, those storylines made this World Cup feel bigger than any single match. Over the course of several weeks, the tournament gave us unforgettable goals, shocking upsets, viral celebrations, heartbreaking exits, and moments that transcended soccer altogether. Here are the moments that defined the 2026 World Cup. Vozinha and the Blue Sharks The best feel-good story of the World Cup has to be Cape Verde's historic run and their goalkeeper, Vozinha (n e Josimar Jos vora Dias). The 40-year-old keeper and Cape Verde stalwart rocketed to international fame after a heroic performance against eventual champions Spain, keeping a clean sheet against the European giants to earn the 500,000-strong nation its first-

title: AT&T loses key ruling in bid to stop offering basic phone service in California
author: Jon Brodkin
body: California can keep enforcing rules that require AT T to offer basic phone service to new customers in its wireline territory, following a federal judge's ruling last week. AT T sued California in May in a bid to end the state's Carrier of Last Resort (COLR) rules that require it to offer telephone service to any potential customer in its territory. AT T asked for a preliminary injunction that would prevent California from enforcing the COLR rules while the litigation continues. To win a preliminary injunction, AT T had to show it is likely to succeed on the merits of its claim that California rules are preempted by a Federal Communications Commission order. US District Judge Linda Lopez denied AT T's request for a preliminary injunction during a motion hearing on Thursday, according to a docket entry . The case is in US District Court for the Southern District of California. Read full article Comments

title: Ford’s $30,000 electric truck: all the news about the company’s big EV re-do
body: The end of the Ford F-150 Lightning was also the start of a new era for the automaker. After failing to capture the market with the electric version of its popular F-Series truck, Ford is going back to the drawing board with its Universal Electric Vehicle Platform. The first offering will be a $30,000 electric truck, engineered for efficiency and also — perhaps most importantly — profitability. The truck will be revealed in 2027, but Ford plans on building a whole family of low-cost electric vehicles at its Kentucky assembly plant. The heavily camouflaged truck has already been spotted out in the wild, with observers noting that it seems even smaller than the Maverick. Success isn’t guaranteed. Ford has struggled to make profitable EVs, forcing the company to take a staggering $19.5 billion hit on its EV investments in 2025. But Ford still sees EVs as the future — just not oversize ones with “no path to profitability,” as executives say. Emojis as camouflage. Ford’s $30K electric truck could be smaller than a Maverick. title: RIP bargain bin: The price impact of Sony's disc-free PlayStation plan
author: Kyle Orland
body: Since Sony announced that it was planning to stop producing physical PlayStation discs in 2028 , many people have been justifiably worried about how they will maintain ownership and long-term game access in a disc-free world. But halting production of PlayStation discs also means eliminating the market for cheap used PlayStation discs, which are often available for well below the price of a digital download. How much is this disc-based discount worth? To find out, Ars analyzed how the prices of 19 current top-selling PlayStation games varied between digital downloads and physical discs (both new and used). We found that, while used discs are the cheapest option for most games for most of the year, the frequent and periodic deep discounts offered on the PlayStation Store often undercut the cheapest game disc options. That state of affairs will likely persist even in a future without PlayStation discs. Our prices are so low, you'll think we've suffered brain damage For our analysis, we started with Sony's list of the bestsellers on the PlayStation Store . We eliminated any games that weren't available on a physical disc, then used the database over at PlatPrices.com to determine both the "Standard" price and the lowest "Discount" price offered for those games on the PlayStation Store in the last year (as well as the total number of days the discount price was offered). Read full article Comments

title: Kalshi gains 3 million users amid World Cup gambling
body: Spain has won the 2026 FIFA World Cup and millions of new Kalshi users either won or lost money betting on the game. According to prediction market Kalshi, the platform brought in 3 million new users during the World Cup tournament, which kicked off on June 11 and ended with the Spain vs. Argentina final on July 19. In addition to the millions of new users, the company said that the World Cup broke a platform record for the most money traded in a single market. Kalshi says more than $1.2 billion in trades, the term the company uses to describe bets, were made to predict the winner of the World Cup. Kalshi had a large presence throughout the World Cup. The company partnered with stars of the sport, such as Luka Modrić of the Croatia national team, who served as a brand ambassador and appeared in a commercial. Kalshi also had a partnership with the Argentina national team and received a featured post for the brand on Lionel Messi's Instagram account, which has over 512 million followers. A commercial featuring A-lister Timoth e Chalamet also appeared frequently in the U.S. market. Despite their constant presence throughout the tournament, neither Kalshi nor its largest competitor, Polymarket, were official sponsors of the FIFA World Cup. A much smaller, new prediction market called ADI Predictstreet was deemed the "Official Prediction Market Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026." However, Kalshi even found a way in there. Kalshi partnered with ADI Predictsteet in co-branded ad placements. In addition, website visitors from markets that ADI Predictstreet were not legally al

title: Jimothy the raccoon is taking over the internet
body: A very unusual raccoon from Seattle has become the internet's newest sweetheart. Since appearing in a brief video filmed in the city s Ballard neighborhood, Jimothy has accumulated millions of views and inspired murals, tattoos, merchandise, government public-service announcements, a city proclamation, and even a surprise appearance at a Seattle Mariners game. Online, fans have promoted him to "King Jimothy," "Saint Jimothy," and Seattle s "sweet prince." "Hot Jimothy Summer," a play on Megan Thee Stallion's "hot girl summer," has become the unofficial slogan surrounding the unusually shaped animal. SEE ALSO: The internet is worried about Punch the monkey. The zoo says he's OK. Jimothy s sudden fame began with one fairly reasonable question: What exactly are we looking at? This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. Meet Jimothy, Seattle's unusually shaped raccoon Kiana Hall encountered Jimothy while walking with her husband through Ballard on July 13. Hall initially thought the animal moving beneath a parked car was a cat and pulled out her phone to get a better look. Then it emerged with the familiar masked face of a raccoon and a much less familiar body shape. Hall posted the video on Instagram and named the raccoon Jimothy, a decision based less on a complicated backstory than an immediate feeling. "He just looked like a 'Jimothy' to me," Hall told TODAY.com . "I don't have any other explanation other than I looked at him and he looked like a 'Jimothy.'" The name stuck. The video garnered millions of views, while people around Ballard b

body: LG’s UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor deliver deep, true-to-life blacks. | Image: LG If you’ve been thinking about upgrading your gaming monitor, LG’s 27-inch 27GX704A-B pairs a glossy WOLED panel with a fast refresh rate, and it’s currently on sale for $379.99 (about $70 off) at Amazon and directly from LG , marking a new low price. It originally launched at $799.99, but has been available for under $500 since May. LG UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor (27-inches) Where to Buy: $449 $379.99 at Amazon $449 $379.99 at LG The monitor offers a 1440p resolution and a glossy coating, which isn’t a combination of specs we often see at this price. Most OLED monitors under $400 use QD-OLED panels, which can give blacks a slight bluish tint. LG’s WOLED monitor delivers truer-looking blacks while the glossy coating helps colors appear richer and more vibrant. It doesn’t sacrifice speed, either. The monitor boasts a fast 240Hz refresh rate with variable refresh rate support (Nvidia G-Sync compatible and AMD FreeSync Premium Pro), along with a fast 0.03ms response time to keep games looking sharp in motion. It comes with a solid array of ports for the price, with two HDMI 2.1 ports, DisplayPort 1.4, and USB-A ports, making it easy to connect a PC, consoles, and accessories. Rounding things out, the stand is fully adjustable, too, allowing you to raise, lower, tilt, swivel, or rotate the display into portrait mode for a more comfortable setup. Or, because it has a VESA mount, you can yank it off and toss it onto a monitor arm of your choice.

title: What does The Odyssey translator Emily Wilson think of Christopher Nolans film?
body: We can't ask Homer what he thinks of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey , but we can ask someone who knows it better than most. Emily Wilson, the British-American classicist whose 2017 translation of the epic poem was one of the director's inspirations , was asked her opinion on The Odyssey film by the Associated Press . "I want to celebrate the fact that so many more people are aware of The Odyssey and were brought back to the poem, she told AP. "But at the same time, I had the sense that Christopher Nolan was trying to pack everything in while missing themes that were essential to the poem." SEE ALSO: 'The Odyssey' hints at 3 myths without detailing them. Here are the untold stories. Nolan has cited Wilson's work as among his influences for the film, notably Homer's opening line, "Tell me about a complicated man." Granted, it's nothing short of a herculean task to try and fit every last element of Homer's 12,000-line poem into a film that runs just under three hours, despite that IMAX screen size . Nolan does hit the major plot points of The Odyssey 's 20-year chronology the Trojan Horse, Penelope's (Anne Hathaway) sleazy suitors, Odysseus (Matt Damon) and his men running into the cyclops Polythemus (Bill Irwin) , the sirens, the Scylla monster, and the nymph Calypso (Charlize Theron) along the way he leaves many myths deliberately untold. Featured Video For You What It Was Like Shooting One of Oppenheimer s Most Remarkable Moments For one thing, Wilson's translation famously reexamined the role of women in The Odyssey , like the characters of Penelope and Circe. And Nolan's

title: F1 in Belgium: Machine learning algorithms are ruining the sport
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Formula 1 returned to the Spa-Francorchamps circuit for the Belgium Grand Prix this weekend. This ribbon of tarmac in the Ardennes is one of the sport's historic venues, featuring in the first F1 season way back in 1950. But as yesterday's race showed, the current F1 machinery is utterly ill-suited to this dramatic venue. As we saw at Suzuka in Japan earlier this year, a 2026 F1 battery is able to power the race car's electric motor for only a few seconds a lap; for much of the rest of the lap, the energy-starved cars have less power going to their rear wheels than in the junior F2 feeder series. The best race track in the world For many, including much of the current F1 grid, Spa is considered perhaps the planet's greatest race track. In fact, both Spa and the Belgian Grand Prix predate the F1 world championship, with its first race held in 1922. Back then, the circuit was a 9-mile loop of public roads, taking in the towns of Malmedy and Stavelot before returning to Francorchamps. In the postwar years, the layout shrank slightly to 8.7 miles (14.1 km) and was ferociously fast, with average lap speeds of up to 150 mph (240 km/h)—and ferociously dangerous, given cars with huge amounts of power and minimal concessions to safety. title: AliExpress hit with record $625M fine after failing to make EU-ordered fixes

body: Yesterday, the European Commission slapped AliExpress with the largest fine yet under the Digital Services Act (DSA), exceeding $625 million. In a press release , the EC said that AliExpress failed to “diligently assess and mitigate risks relating to the sale of illegal, unsafe, or counterfeit products on its e-commerce platform.” Officials found that AliExpress not only failed to staff teams dedicated to removing counterfeit and dangerous items from the platform, but it also made it easy for bad actors to evade detection. title: Dr. Jill Lepore on why AI backlash is vital for the future
body: Today, I’m talking with Harvard professor and New Yorker staff writer Dr. Jill Lepore about her new book, The Rise and Fall of the Artificial State , which comes out on August 25. Jill is one of the best writers there is at identifying institutional patterns in history, and Decoder is a show about systems, so this book could not possibly be more in our zone. You build systems by measuring things — gathering information, quantifying it as data, and taking action on it. And Jill’s big thesis in the book is that this kind of systemic measurement is really a dehumanizing act, one that flattens human concerns into something, well, artificial. These artificial structures touch everything — not just in the more obvious places like in apps or AI, but all the way up to politics and governance itself, and Jill’s insights here are a telling portrait of the way Big Tech and its billionaire figureheads have managed to break the very idea of the state itself. Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Decoder wherever you get your podcasts. Head here . Not a subscriber? You can sign up here . You’ll hear Jill tell me she wanted to write a “kind of grumpy book,” but in my reading, it’s a pretty dead-ahead look at the last century and a half of technological and social developments and how we got to a place where bots are maybe running everything. Jill’s idea of the artificial state starts with the very idea of quantification, from the first stabs at taking a census thousands of years ago to the invention of modern political polling in the 1930s. But where sh

(Spoilers for prior relevant MCU films below.) Marvel Studios originally planned to build its Phase Six Avengers arc ( The Kang Dynasty ) around Jonathan Majors’ Kang the Conqueror (and associated variants), introduced in Loki and Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania . But then Majors was convicted of domestic violence, and Marvel fired the actor soon after. That meant the studio needed to retool its Phase Six plans, culminating in the announced return of the Russo brothers, who directed four of the Marvel Cinematic Universe’s most successful films, which brought in more than $6 billion at the global box office. title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices
author: Hannah Murphy, Financial Times
body: Nearly half of companies that are targets of a ransomware cyber attack end up paying a ransom to release their data or systems, according to 2025 research from cyber security group Sophos, while the median amount demanded is rising. Globally, some jurisdictions are responding by banning payments to hackers. In the UK, for example, the government is advancing plans to prohibit public sector bodies and critical national infrastructure groups—including the National Health Service, local councils and schools—from making payouts. The potential veto comes as ransomware hackers have become more advanced and meticulous in their targeting of companies, particularly vulnerable small and medium-sized businesses, over time. While many GPS smartwatches with offline maps can cost hundreds of dollars, the Watch Hyper launches at $85, with a $9.01 preorder discount automatically applied at checkout. The company says the Watch Hyper is built for people who split their time between city life and outdoor adventures, combining navigation tools, fitness tracking, and everyday smartwatch features into a single device. One of the Watch Hyper's standout features is its L1 + L5 dual-band GPS, which improves location accuracy whether you're navigating busy city streets or hiking on trails. It also includes offline maps, full-color topographic maps, and route backtracking, so you can find your way even without a cellular connection. The smartwatch features a 1.97-inch AMOLED display with adaptive brightness, making it easy to see your screen in bright outdoor conditions. If fitness is your thing, the Watch Hyper supports more than 170 sports modes and offers features like smart workout detection, VO₂ Max estimates, personalized training goals, and workout insights powered by Haylou's Sevier motion algorithm. title: English footballer Wayne Rooneys blunt reaction to World Cup halftime show goes viral
body: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final on Sunday was something of a first for the tournament, in that it featured a halftime show similar to the kind you typically associate with the Super Bowl . Coldplay's Chris Martin curated the MetLife Stadium show with Global Citizen and he certainly went all out, bringing together performances from the likes of Shakira, BTS, Justin Bieber, and Burna Boy , a cameo from Ted Lasso (Jason Sudeikis) himself, and a special appearance from the Muppets. SEE ALSO: T-Mobile is handing over the Apple iPhone 17 for free how to claim yours Needless to say the internet reacted , and there was plenty of excitement from most corners. But not all. Watching on from the BBC Sport commentary box, it's fair to say former England footballer-turned-pundit Wayne Rooney was less than impressed with the spectacle. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. Responding to host Gabby Logan's question to name his favorite bit of the halftime show, Rooney remained straight-faced as he gave the following response: "I'll be honest, mine was when it finished. I like a lot of them artists, but I thought it was crap." That clip has now been shared thousands of times on X. You certainly can't fault his honesty. title: Samsungs new 990 SSD has dropped — features, price, and where to buy
body: TL;DR: Samsung's new 990 SSD is now available with 1TB ($269.99) and 2TB ($529.99) . The PCIe Gen 4 NVMe drive promises read speeds of up to 7,250 MB/s and write speeds of up to 6,450 MB/s. Opens in a new window Credit: Samsung Samsung 990 SSD $269.99 at Amazon Shop Now We know upgrading your PC hasn't been exactly cheap lately. Between RAMageddon and electronics price hikes across the board, everything from SSDs to laptops have become more expensive in 2026. But if you're looking to add more storage, Samsung has a new option on the table. Samsung has officially launched the Samsung 990 SSD, a PCIe Gen 4 NVMe drive built for everyday users who want fast, responsive storage for gaming, creative work, and daily computing. Available now in 1TB ($269.99) and 2TB ($529.99) capacities, the new drive focuses on delivering high performance while using less power than Samsung's flagship 990 Pro. What does the Samsung 990 SSD offer? The Samsung 990 SSD is designed to speed up everyday tasks, whether you're loading games, moving large files, or managing creative projects. It delivers sequential read speeds of up to 7,250 MB/s and write speeds of up to 6,450 MB/s, helping reduce load times, speed up installations, and transfer high-resolution photos and videos quickly. Samsung says the drive reaches random read and write speeds of up to 850K and 1,200K IOPS, which can help keep everyday workloads running smoothly and responsively. The new drive is positioned as an easy upgrade for existing PCs or a dependable option for new desktop builds. More speed without the price tag Beyond raw sp

title: BTS met IShowSpeed at the World Cup final and its everything
body: BTS and IShowSpeed met each other in the hallway at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final and the video is pure glee with added backflip. The South Korean superstars performed in the first ever World Cup halftime show on Sunday, during the final match between victors Spain and runners up Argentina. In a delightful exchange on the day, BTS members RM, Jin, SUGA, j-hope, Jimin, V, and Jung Kook ran into the YouTube megastar (and Mashable 101 alumnus ) in the halls of MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. SEE ALSO: English footballer Wayne Rooney's blunt reaction to World Cup halftime show goes viral In a video posted to Instagram by IShowSpeed, it's hard to tell who is fanning out harder. View this post on Instagram Speed's backflip? So casual. And there's a lot of "big fan" exclamations exchanged all round. It's extremely sweet. "We were just as amazed to meet you🙌" wrote BTS' V in the comments. J-Hope and RM also added emoji under the video. During the World Cup halftime show , BTS performed their 2020 Grammy nominated, chart-topping, YouTube-smashing single "Dynamite," the group's first number one in America. In a performance curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin and Global Citizen, BTS shared the lineup with Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira, Burna Boy, the Muppets, and more, including appearances from football legends Ronaldo and Ronaldinho and Ted Lasso 's Jason Sudeikis and Brendan Hunt in character. BTS will perform again at the MetLife Stadium in a few weeks' time, returning to the U.S. for more dates on their ARIRANG World Tour . Featured Video For You How F1 b

title: Samsungs Galaxy Fold-inspired pub is coming to London — what to expect
body: TL;DR: Samsung is opening The Fold Inn, a limited-time Galaxy Fold-inspired pop-up pub in London, on July 24-25. The venue will feature a fold-themed bar, custom drinks inspired by Samsung's latest Galaxy Z devices , and hands-on experiences with the company's newest foldable phones . If you're in London this month, Samsung wants to treat you to a foldable-inspired pub experience. The tech giant has announced The Fold Inn, a limited-time pop-up pub in London inspired by its Galaxy Fold lineup. Blending classic British pub culture with Samsung's foldable design language, the immersive space offers visitors a fresh way to experience the company's latest Galaxy Z devices . What is The Fold Inn? The Fold Inn isn't just a themed bar it's part product showcase, part pub experience. title: Apple Music will now cost you more
body: Apple has increased the subscription price of Apple Music in the U.S., but that's not where the price hikes end. An Apple Music Individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, up from $10.99. Student plans now cost $6.99, up from $5.99. And if you want to get a Family plan, that'll be $19.99, up from $16.99. Europeans get the exact same numbers, only in euros: Individual plans are now 11.99, Student plans are 6.99, and a Family plan is 19.99. In the UK, the Individual plan now costs 11.99, while the Family plan is 19.99. New subscribers will have to pay the new prices right away; existing subscribers will get a notification soon and will be billed the new prices at their next billing cycle. In a statement to Music Business Worldwide , Apple said the price hikes are a result of "rising licensing costs." SEE ALSO: Apple price increases arrive: How much MacBooks and iPads will cost you now Apple One plans have also gotten pricier. While the basic plan still has the same price, the Family plan now costs $27.95 (up from $25.95), while the Premier plan costs $39.95 (up from $37.95). There's more bad news for consumers, although it's scattered throughout different markets. As MacRumors noticed , iPhone prices have gone up roughly 8-10 percent in Japan, and iCloud+ prices have gone up quite a bit (from 11 to 55 percent) in 8 countries: Nigeria, Turkey, Vietnam, Japan, Egypt, New Zealand, the Philippines, and Indonesia. The news comes about a month after Apple increased the prices of numerous products in its lineup, mostly due to the memory chip supply crunch.

title: Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup halftime show with Shakira, BTS, and Justin Bieber
body: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final kicked off on Sunday evening at MetLife Stadium, New Jersey, with Argentina and Spain battling it out for the championship title. This year's tournament was a little different though, with FIFA staging its first halftime show in the competition's 96-year history. SEE ALSO: The internet reacts to the first-ever World Cup Halftime show Curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin, the FIFA World Cup halftime show was an 11-minute gauntlet of musicians from across the globe, including Shakira , BTS , Justin Bieber , and Burna Boy. The Muppets also played an instrumental rendition of The White Stripes' "Seven Nation Army" with the New York Philharmonic orchestra. Madonna opened the show with a mashup of "Music" and "Danceteria," with Brazilian soccer greats Ronaldo and Ronaldinho appearing alongside her, while K-pop phenomenon BTS performed a truncated version of their English-language hit "Dynamite." Ted Lasso actors Jason Sudeikis and Brendan Hunt then made cameos, respectively playing the title character and Coach Beard from the feel-good football series, and giving Justin Bieber a pep talk before he slowed things down with an acoustic performance of "Everything Hallelujah." Shakira and Burna Boy followed with the 2026 FIFA World Cup's official anthem "Dai Dai," delivering a jarring change of mood, before before Coldplay, the Muppets, and the PS22 Chorus children's choir closed out the halftime show by debuting the band's new song "We Dance." The full performance is available now on FIFA's official YouTube channel . Unfortunately, despite the star-studded

title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 20, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. Mashable 101 Fan Fave: Nominate your favorite creators today If you find yourself stuck at any step of today's Hurdle, don't worry! We have you covered. title: Moon phase today: What the Moon will look like on July 20
body: Today is International Moon Day, a day dedicated to celebrating the role the Moon has played in our understanding of space. Held each year on July 20, the event commemorates the historic Apollo 11 mission and the first time humans set foot on the Moon. Keep reading to see what you can spot on the Moon's surface tonight. What is today s Moon phase? As of Monday, July 20, NASA's Daily Moon Guide tracker tells us the Moon phase is in Waxing Crescent phase, with 38% of its surface visible. You won't need any visual aids to be able to spot the Mares Crisium and Fecunditatis. Binoculars will also help you to see the Endymion Crater, Posidonius Crater, and the Mare Nectaris. A telescope will of course widen your visibility, with areas like the Apollo 17 and 11 landing spot, the perfect way to celebrate International Moon Day. When is the next Full Moon? The next Full Moon will take place on July 29. What are Moon phases? The Moon completes one full cycle around Earth in about 29.5 days, moving through eight different phases along the way, NASA explains. Although the same side of the Moon always faces our planet, the amount of sunlight reflecting off its surface changes as it travels around Earth. This changing angle of illumination is what makes the Moon appear to transform throughout the month, from a thin crescent to a half-lit Moon and eventually a fully illuminated Full Moon, before starting the cycle again. New Moon - The Moon is between Earth and the sun, so the side we see is dark (in other words, it's invisible to the eye). Waxing Crescent - A small sliver of light appears

title: NYT Pips hints, answers for July 20, 2026
body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 20, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

title: The Vampire Lestat: Burning questions following The Failures finale
body: As promised from the first glimpse of the aspiring rock star Brat Prince, The Vampire Lestat has been a wild ride. The third season of Rolin Jones' Interview with the Vampire was a chaotic odyssey led by Lestat de Lioncourt ( Sam Reid ), as he reconnected with his long-lost love Louis de Pointe du Lac (Jacob Anderson), antagonized his documentarian Daniel Molloy (Eric Bogosian), faced the wrath of his departed daughter Claudia (Delainey Hayles), taunted his ex Armand (Assad Zaman), hooked up with his fledgling/mom Sofia/Gabriella (Jennifer Ehle), and awakened Akasha, aka the Queen of the Damned (Sheila Atim). However, the finale episode titled "The Failures," after Lestat's recorded telling of his life and times didn't put to rest all of the many, juicy threads unearthed. So, here's what we expect to see more of in Season 2. How did Nicki die? In interviews with Mashable, Reid teased that this season was largely about Lestat running from his past trauma rather than confronting it. In episode 3, Lestat told Daniel via a telepathy trick about how Armand threw Nicki into a fire, and the horrid "O" sound his lover made as he perished. But in the finale, that same moan returns as Lestat remembers seeing the alleged witches burned when he was just a boy. Reid told Mashable , "And then you see [Lestat] reprocess that memory as his own memory, watching these women die, and understanding for the first time: Yes, Gabriella has abused him, and has assaulted him throughout his whole life." This means Nicki's death which was notably not shown in the series didn't go down exactly as Lest

title: Sam Reid reveals the major obstacles of The Vampire Lestat: One Night Only – LIVE
body: With The Vampire Lestat, Interview with a Vampire fans witnessed Lestat de Lioncourt (Sam Reid) pursue his rock star ambitions in a bloody and wild tour across North America. In real life, Reid got a taste of the attention his character craves with The Vampire Lestat: One Night Only - LIVE . As advertised, Reid performed songs from The Vampire Lestat written by Daniel Hart before a live audience for one night only. Mashable was there. In my review of the event , I wrote, "Reid's onstage persona evoked David Bowie, Mick Jagger, and Steven Tyler, as he crooned, strutted, flipped his hair, flung a scarf, and generally oozed sex appeal with a crooked grin." But, what was it like for the man behind the mic? Trickier than you might have dreamed. In my latest interview with Reid, he dug into The Vampire Lestat 's finale , where the series might go next , and what it was like for him to take to the stage in character as Lestat. "It's funny," Reid said, "These things it's very work for me, you know what I mean? I am not a touring musician. I'm an actor, and so I'm playing a character. I'm going out there, promoting a show. It was wonderful to be able to play that music with those musicians, namely Daniel Hart. I'm really proud of his music, and I think he worked his ass off in this season. So I'm really proud to share it." Reid went on: "It's quite easy to play Lestat for me, you know. So there was a level of ease about it, because I'm not having to go out and share my own personal music and bear my soul." This distance allowed Reid to focus on his character over any nerves about pe

title: Craving The Vampire Lestat Season 2? Sam Reid gave us some idea of whats coming next.
body: Well, when it came to predicting how Season 1 of The Vampire Lestat would end, I was way, way off. Show creator Rolin Jones went way off-book from Anne Rice's source material, crafting a fascinating, mind-bending finale. While the decapitated Louis de Pointe du Lac (Jacob Anderson) had to face his ex-lover Armand (Assad Zaman), Lestat de Lioncourt ( Sam Reid ) had to face the long table and its Failure Finders. Thankfully, both survived their brushes with death. But in the finale's final scene, Lestat reveals the ravaged world sparked by the concert that the episode skipped over. We've got burning questions. But in an interview with Mashable, Reid dropped some hints on what's next for the Interview with the Vampire series. What's coming up in The Vampire Lestat Season 2? Over a video call with me, Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko, Reid noted that the next season of Interview with a Vampire will move beyond Anne Rice's second novel, for which this season was named, The Vampire Lestat . "The way that [the show's writers] pull from the books," Reid explained, "we're not necessarily just doing The Vampire Lestat . We're looking at multiple books here in the overall arc particularly those next three books." Those would be The Queen of the Damned, The Tale of the Body Thief, and Memnoch the Devil. In the first of these, the central narrator conceit is abandoned, as Rice took on the perspective of a variety of characters, including Baby Jenks . The focus of the overall story is the rise of Akasha (Sheila Atim) and the devastation she wreaks upon the world of mortals and

title: The Vampire Lestat finale: Sam Reid on witches burning and poor Nicki
body: Early on in The Vampire Lestat 's first season, the eponymous anti-hero mentions a formatively traumatic moment that we finally see in full in the finale, titled "The Failures." When Lestat de Lioncourt (Sam Reid) was just a boy, his mother, Gabriella (Jennifer Ehle), took him to see their neighbors burn alleged witches alive. In the final episode of this season, Lestat finally stops deflecting about what this moment meant. At the long table, before the Failure Finders , he confronts his resentment at his mother for how this execution impacted him for the next 200-plus years. In a post-mortem interview, Sam Reid told Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko what that scene means for The Vampire Lestat and how it connects to the final moments of Nicolas de Lenfent (Joseph Potter). What did the witches burning mean to Lestat? Featured Video For You 'The Vampire Lestat's Sam Reid on Magnus, Claudia, Sofia, and more In episode 7 of The Vampire Lestat , the version of Gabriella at the long table argues that she took the young child Lestat to witness the witches burn so that he would see his neighbors for the vicious and ignorant "cabbages" they are. She wanted him to be motivated to leave this terrible place for something better. Asked about this sequence, Reid told Mashable, "That was one of the main conversations I had with [showrunner] Rolin [Jones] about the whole Lestat journey this season, was getting to the core of him, [which] was this existential crisis that he had when he was a child." "You know, he [initially] puts it on Nicki, because he cannot deal with that," Re

title: The Vampire Lestat finale: Sam Reid reveals why Paul is back
body: When Sam Reid first sat down with Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko, they dug into the first three episodes of The Vampire Lestat , focusing on the Brat Prince's perspective on his makers and f emale fledglings . Now, in a post-mortem interview, he's dug into the season's mind-snapping finale, the Failure Finders, what's next for Lestat , and why Paul de Pointe du Lac (Steven G. Norfleet) makes a surprise appearance. In episode 7, titled "The Failures," Lestat faces the long table. There, after facing down those he loves and loathes most, he turns it into a stage, where he performs for his long line of victims. But as his time is running out, and it seems his death is coming, it's Louis' long-gone brother Paul who died by his own volition and not by Lestat's fangs who comes to share a quiet moment with him. Reid reflected on what this scene means to Lestat. Why does Paul show up at the long table? Featured Video For You 'The Vampire Lestat's Sam Reid on Magnus, Claudia, Sofia, and more Reid says of this sequence, "These people at the concert are the ones who he has traumatized in their life, or he has killed. But Paul represents the reason why Louis can't say 'I love you' to him." In the scene, Paul seems to come from the afterlife as Claudia did, but with no wrath to meet Lestat, and offer a word about what he's realized about his brother. In death (or near it), the religious zealot and the Brat Prince are able to empathize with Louis in a way they couldn't before. "The reason why [Lestat] can't get the validation that he needs from the person he wants it from th

title: Sam Reid on The Vampire Lestats finale and the Failure Finders
body: After much fucking about, Lestat hit his find out era. In episode 7, titled "The Failures," while a decapitated Louis de Pointe du Lac (Jacob Anderson) is tortured by a bitter Armand (Assad Zaman), Lestat is forced to face his past. We finally see the long table teased all season long in the opening credits. Earlier this year, Reid came by Mashable's studio for a Say More interview , where he talked Claudia , Magnus , and much more. Now, he and Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko reconnected for a postmortem of the Brat Prince's finale. Who are the Failure Finders at the long table? At the head of the table sits Lestat's maker, Magnus (Damien Atkins). Then around the table, his fledglings, Nicolas "Nicki" de Lenfent (Joseph Potter), Louis, Gabriella (Jennifer Ehle), Antoinette (Maura Grace), and his bandmate TC (Sarah Swire). But no Claudia (Bailey Bass). As Lestat's severed head is dying, who are these figures? Are they ghosts of those he's loved? But how so, when some are still alive? Are they the voices in his head? Something supernatural from the savage garden? "I don't think [Lestat] knows." Reid told Mashable, "But he knows that they are coming. The whole season's been set up for this sequence. [In episode 1] Baby Jenks says, 'You know, they're coming for you.' Who are they? And then Magnus in the car [in episode 3] is like, 'At the long table, we eat your soul.' So, he knows that they are coming the long table is coming." He went on, "I think when Lestat arrives there [at the long table], because [he is] a supernatural being, there's a lot of space to accept

title: House of the Dragon Season 3, episode 5s shocking ending is way different in the book
body: Just two episodes after Queen Rhaenyra Targaryen (Emma D'Arcy) threw a nauseating rat banquet , House of the Dragon upped its gross food game with another disgusting feast. The feast shows up in Season 3, episode 5, which reveals that Ormund Hightower (James Norton) has been bankrolling the murders of Gold Cloaks across King's Landing. By the episode's end, Daemon Targaryen (Matt Smith) and Ser Luthor Largent (Tom Cullen) have killed one man who was involved in the killings, and who claims he was acting alone. However, that turns out not to the case, because by the episode's end, nine other Gold Cloaks are dead, including Luthor. SEE ALSO: 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 family tree: How the Targaryens, Hightowers, and Velaryons connect Daemon discovers these corpses posed in a horrifying tableau. The deceased Gold Cloaks sit around a long table, their innards splayed out among tankards and candles. Above them, someone has painted the words "a feast for traitors" in blood. It's a disgusting sequence, a cross between "The Last Supper" and body horror. It's also not in House of the Dragon 's source material, George R.R. Martin's Fire and Blood at least, not in this exact way. For starters, the Gold Cloak killing spree is a show-only addition, as are all of Ormund's plans to crown Daeron Targaryen (Benjamin Evan Ainsworth). However, the corpse feast does take place, just in a very different setting. In Fire and Blood , as Ser Criston Cole (Fabien Frankel) and his men march south through the riverlands, his scouts come across a similar corpse feast. Pro-Rhaenyra forces staged a

SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 20, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? title: House of the Dragon Season 3, episode 5: Who is Lady Sabitha Frey?
body: Rhaenyra's Small Council in House of the Dragon is growing larger by the day. The latest addition? Lady Sabitha Frey (Sarah Woodward), who appears to have been given a position mostly to compensate for Rhaenyra not giving her the already-promised Harrenhal. "I had hoped a seat on the Small Council would suffice as recompense for House Frey's loyalty," Rhaenyra says, power-walking her way through the halls of the Red Keep. "But Harrenhal was to be our recompense, payment for giving your Winter Wolves passage across the twins, promised by your own son!" snaps back Lady Sabitha. She's eventually silenced by a withering look from Rhaenyra, and reluctantly accepts her seat. But hang on a minute, who is Lady Sabitha Frey again? And what exactly was the deal Team Black made with the Freys? Let's recap. SEE ALSO: 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 family tree: How the Targaryens, Hightowers, and Velaryons connect Who is Lady Sabitha Frey? Lady Sabitha Frey is the head of House Frey. It's a while back now, but we have met her before. In Season 2, episode 5, Jace (Harry Collett) meets with her and her husband, Lord Forrest Frey (Kenneth Collard), to cut a deal with them. Credit: Ollie Upton / HBO During the meeting, Lady Sabitha Frey brings up the possibility of Harrenhal, to which Jace (RIP) says, "For that, Mother will want more than your crossing." "What does her grace desire?" asks Sabitha. "Bent knees," is Jace's response. Flash back to Season 3, and you can kind of see why Lady Frey isn't too happy. As she reminds Rhaenyra, the Freys allowed the Starks to use their crossing. But in

title: House of the Dragon Season 3, episode 5 brings a character back from the dead
body: There's a rich tradition of characters coming back from the dead in the House of the Dragon universe. We've already seen it happen with Lord Corlys (Steve Toussaint), but now it appears that the Battle of the Gullet has unclaimed yet another victim. So, who else didn't die, and how did they make it out alive? SEE ALSO: 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 family tree: How the Targaryens, Hightowers, and Velaryons connect What happens with Tyland Lannister in Season 3, episode 5? After giving Aegon (Tom Glynn-Carney) a well-deserved tongue-lashing, Larys Strong (Matthew Needham) finally manages to convince him to board a ship to Essos and give up on the foolish idea of tracking down his brother Aemond (Ewan Mitchell). But just as they've come to this agreement, they hear a voice: "What ship might that be?" "Seven hells," responds Larys when he realises that the cloaked man in front of them is Tyland Lannister (Jefferson Hall), who was last seen being yeeted over the side of a different ship by Admiral Lohar (Abigail Thorn). So, how did he survive, and what's he doing there? Featured Video For You 'House of the Dragon' cast unpack Season 3, episode 2's biggest moments How did Tyland Lannister survive? We only get one more brief scene with Aegon, Larys, and Tyland in episode 5, but it does give us a little more information to work with. We don't know how Tyland managed to swim to safety wearing all that armour, or how he's managed to trek however many miles it is from where he washed ashore to where Larys and Aegon have ended up but we do know that he's probably managed to use the L

title: House of the Dragon Season 3, episode 5: Who is killing the Gold Cloaks in Kings Landing?
body: All men must die, but apparently that goes double if you're wearing a gold cloak. House of the Dragon Season 3, episode 5 sees an already-embattled Rhaenyra (Emma D'Arcy) contending with a new threat from inside King's Landing: The systematic murder of her City Watch, who are having their throats and bowels slashed open at an alarming rate. So, who is doing it, and why? Let's unpack. SEE ALSO: 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 family tree: How the Targaryens, Hightowers, and Velaryons connect What happens to the Gold Cloaks in Season 3, episode 5? "Two of our Gold Cloaks were found dead in the streets this morning," says Daemon (Matt Smith) during a Small Council meeting. "Put to the blade by an unknown hand." He's keen to get to the bottom of the problem himself, but while meeting with Ser Luthor (Tom Cullen), who heads up the Gold Cloaks, he finds two more of their number dead. This time it's happened in broad daylight, and they can't seem to find witnesses. Later, just as Daemon has finished giving a speech to try and rouse the surviving Gold Cloaks, he gets word that someone has been apprehended. Under torture, the man confesses but he says he acted alone, and never saw the face of the man who paid him. "He said it was the king's work I was to be about," the man rasps. title: India's first privately-developed rocket reaches orbit on dramatic debut launch
author: Stephen Clark
body: Indian space officials celebrated the debut flight of Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket, India's first fully commercial satellite launcher, as a "grand success" Saturday after an on-target climb into a 280-mile-high orbit following liftoff from an island spaceport in the Bay of Bengal. The Vikram-1 lifted off from India's primary spaceport on Sriharikota Island at 1:35 am EDT (06:35 UTC) Saturday, around midday at the launch base along India's southeast coast. The launch was delayed more than a half-hour to resolve a last-minute technical problem. The countdown resumed, culminating in the command to ignite Vikram-1's solid-fueled first stage booster to propel the rocket off the launch pad. Vikram-1 is modest in size compared to India's larger workhorse rockets. Skyroot's rocket stands about 72 feet (22 meters) tall, with the capability to place payloads of up to 770 pounds (350 kilograms) into low-Earth orbit. This makes Vikram-1 somewhat larger than the Electron launch vehicle developed by Rocket Lab, the world's most successful dedicated small satellite launcher. Read full article Comments

title: The internet reacts to the first-ever World Cup Halftime show
body: The FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina is living up to the hype. And to add to the festivities was a fun but odd sight in the world of association football: a halftime show. Soccer is normally a brisk sport, but with this edition of the World Cup being co-hosted in the US (the first World Cup in the US since 1994), it's fallen into the American Sports Entertainment Vortex . Which isn't a bad thing, minus the universal dislike for the hydration breaks, which many commentators have pointed out are glorified commercial breaks for the Fox network. But given the lineup Shakira, Madonna, BTS, and Justin Bieber, with a Sesame Street cameo and a Ted Lasso intro thrown in for good measure the World Cup's first-ever halftime show was just fine . The whole thing clocked in at under six minutes, which somehow made it funnier: FIFA spent months hyping a halftime show shorter than the ad break before it. The internet noticed and acted accordingly. Madonna kicks us off Madonna's entrance into the World Cup halftime was mostly prerecorded as she sang from her new album Confessions II . The EC35 certainly isn't fancy. Its 25mm acrylic lens with a fixed f/10 aperture and 1/100 shutter speed basically put it on par with a drugstore disposable. It's one of my favorite music gear purchases. Electric chord organs let you play full chords with just a press of a button, making them perfect for aspiring singer-songwriters or fooling around at home. title: Lawsuit says AI tool disproportionately places Black prisoners in maximum security in Ontario
body: An artificial intelligence program used to classify prisoners' security levels in Ontario jails is disproportionately placing Black prisoners in maximum-security conditions, according to an investigation published by The Breach and a class action lawsuit filed against the province. The reporting, by journalist Desmond Cole, examined the Security Assessment for Evaluating Risk (SAFER) tool, which has been used in Ontario jails since 2021 to assign prisoners a risk score based on factors including arrest and disciplinary history. Citing government data analyzed by University of Toronto criminologist Scot Wortley, the outlet reported that Black people make up roughly 5 percent of Ontario's population but represented close to 27 percent of prisoners in maximum security between 2022 and 2025, while white people were significantly underrepresented in that category relative to their share of the population. The class action , filed by law firm Koskie Minsky, alleges the province was aware the tool's reliance on historical justice-system data which reflects documented racial disparities in policing and charging could disproportionately harm Black prisoners, and that Ontario adopted mitigation measures for Indigenous prisoners without extending similar safeguards to Black prisoners. Internal ministry training documents cited by The Breach acknowledged that "assessments like SAFER would likely contribute to the overrepresentation" of Indigenous and racialized people in maximum security. The Breach reported that neither Ontario's Ministry of the Solicitor General nor Grant Duwe, the r

title: France orders Polymarket blocked over illegal gambling promotion
body: France's National Gaming Authority (ANJ) has ordered the country's internet service providers to block access to Polymarket , the crypto-based prediction market. This decision came after determining the platform was illegally promoting gambling and games of chance to French users. The blocking order, issued July 16 by the ANJ's president, applies to all Polymarket sites operated by ADVENTURE ONE QSS INC. SEE ALSO: Spotify deletes 500K streams of popular song for suspected Kalshi, Polymarket user manipulation The regulator says it has tracked Polymarket's activity in France since November 2024. An earlier formal notice prompted the company to geoblock financial transactions from French users, but the ANJ said that the restriction was being circumvented. The agency noted that prediction markets are not authorized under French law and had already warned the public in February that using such platforms carries risks, including addiction and market manipulation. The ANJ also pointed to an active investigation, opened in May by the Paris prosecutor's cybercrime unit, into allegations that weather-related bets on the platform were manipulated through compromised sensors. The regulator said Polymarket lacks a "Know Your Customer" identification system for its French and European users. France joins a growing list of European countries including Belgium, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Spain, and Switzerland that have restricted or blocked prediction markets. The ANJ said it blocked 1,290 URLs in 2025 under its administrative powers. The action follows other recent controversies fo

title: As mosquito ranges expand, better monitoring is key to preventing disease
author: Madeline Shaw, Inside Climate News
body: With summer heat comes pool parties, beach days, backyard cookouts, and, of course, swarms of bloodthirsty mosquitoes. But while insect bites have always been a side effect of time spent outdoors, the species doing the biting are changing in historically temperate regions like New England. As climate change makes these areas warmer and wetter, their ranges are expanding—and any diseases they carry come with them. In Connecticut, for example, a statewide mosquito monitoring program has detected 54 different species, including invasives like the Asian tiger mosquito, which can transmit potentially serious diseases including dengue and Zika . The mosquito’s historical territory is in hot and humid climates farther south, but it has been moving north . Read full article Comments

title: 25 years later, the Final Fantasy X opening song still rocks
body: Around the turn of the millennium, straight edge hardcore singer Bill Muir was asked by Japanese game developer Square (now known as Square Enix) to lend his vocals to a song for its upcoming RPG, Final Fantasy X . It seemed like just another recording session. Instead, it became the opening track to one of the most beloved games in the series. Twenty-five years later, "Otherworld" remains one of the most iconic video game openings ever made. At first, Final Fantasy X lulls players into a familiar rhythm. Gentle music plays over the opening menus, evoking the orchestral soundscapes longtime fans had come to expect from the series. Then, just minutes into the game, Muir's ferocious vocals rip through the silence. As Tidus awakens and flashes back to his former life as a blitzball star in Zanarkand, "Otherworld" explodes onto the soundtrack, replacing tranquility with pounding guitars and raw aggression. It's a startling introduction that immediately announces Final Fantasy X isn't interested in playing by the series' established rules. Muir, who was in his late twenties at the time, was living in Tokyo and performing with his band, xtillidiex (pronounced "Till I Die"), when he was asked to work on the song. A few months after the game's July 19, 2001, release in Japan, Muir got a taste of how much people loved the track. "I remember being at a show, a later show for my band xtillidiex, as we were wrapping up and I was going to be leaving Japan, somebody having that song on as a ringtone, Muir told Mashable over a video call from Los Angeles. "And I remember slowly turning an

title: World Cup halftime show today: What time to watch Justin Bieber, BTS, Madonna, and Shakira live
body: FIFA is giving the World Cup final its first Super Bowl-style halftime show , and the lineup is almost as crowded as the pitch. Justin Bieber, BTS, Madonna, and Shakira will headline the performance during Sunday s final between Spain and Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium . Burna Boy, conductor Gustavo Dudamel, and the PS22 Chorus featuring Coldplay will also appear, along with characters from Sesame Street and The Muppets . Coldplay frontman Chris Martin curated the show. What time is the World Cup halftime show? The World Cup final kicks off Sunday, July 19, at 3 p.m. ET, and the halftime show should begin sometime around 3:45 to 3:50 p.m. ET , assuming the match starts on time and there are no major delays. FIFA and Global Citizen have not announced an exact start time for the performance because, unlike the Super Bowl, soccer does not stop at a perfectly scheduled point. The first half lasts 45 minutes, but additional stoppage time will push halftime back by several minutes. Viewers primarily interested in the music should tune in before 3:45 or risk missing the opening of the show. The performance itself will last 11 minutes, but the full halftime break could reportedly be considerably longer since crews will need time to build and remove the stage. Broadcasters are preparing for the usual 15-minute interval to stretch to as long as 30 minutes. Not everyone is thrilled about that possibility, especially given the sport s history. title: NYT Pips hints, answers for July 19, 2026
body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 19, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

title: Googles AI Search is a minefield for schools
body: Millennials, the first computer generation, recall many a classroom lecture about the unreliability of Wikipedia. We were told to be wary of unverified forum posts. We were advised to seek out primary sources on the World Wide Web, and the place to start was Google. Now, as Gen Alpha enters AI-powered classrooms, can Google Search no longer be trusted? A new report by child safety watchdog Common Sense Media concluded that Google's modernized, AI-powered Search poses an "unacceptable risk" to young Googlers. In fact, the report authors say, the generative AI search engine is so risky that students should stop using it completely until Google educators can turn the AI off. SEE ALSO: Teens talking to Meta AI about suicide will trigger alert Google's AI Search as classroom threat Child safety advocates have been urging schools to ditch classroom technology for years. As generative AI embeds itself in educational products, those cries have gotten louder. But while it's easy to see the potential harm in introducing something like ChatGPT to young students, it may not be as obvious that a simple Google search exposes young learners to AI. Google launched AI Mode for Search last year. Unlike AI Overviews' rollout, AI Mode was available to everyone, immediately, including education accounts. Both Common Sense Media and other experts Mashable spoke to said this was alarming, primarily because Google's AI Mode is powered by Gemini models models that are age-gated in other Google experiences, including on Google's educational technology suite. Google Search is a tentpole product for t

title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 19, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. title: Moon phase today: What the Moon will look like on July 19
body: Wondering what you're looking at on the Moon's surface? Wonder no more, with the help of NASA's Daily Moon Guide , we've broken down all the features you might be looking at when you look up tonight. What is today s Moon phase? As of Sunday, July 19, NASA's Daily Moon Guide tracker tells us the Moon phase is in Waxing Crescent phase, with 29% of its surface visible. You won't need any visual aids to be able to spot the Mares Crisium and Fecunditatis. However, if you do have binoculars or a telescope, you'll also be able to see the Endymion Crater. When is the next Full Moon? The next Full Moon will take place on July 29. What are Moon phases? The Moon completes one full cycle around Earth in about 29.5 days, moving through eight different phases along the way, NASA explains. Although the same side of the Moon always faces our planet, the amount of sunlight reflecting off its surface changes as it travels around Earth. This changing angle of illumination is what makes the Moon appear to transform throughout the month, from a thin crescent to a half-lit Moon and eventually a fully illuminated Full Moon, before starting the cycle again. New Moon - The Moon is between Earth and the sun, so the side we see is dark (in other words, it's invisible to the eye). Waxing Crescent - A small sliver of light appears on the right side (Northern Hemisphere). First Quarter - Half of the Moon is lit on the right side. It looks like a half-Moon. Waxing Gibbous - More than half is lit up, but it s not quite full yet. Full Moon - The whole face of the Moon is illuminated and fully visible. Wani

title: NYT Strands hints, answers for July 19, 2026
body: Today's NYT Strands hints are easy if you like things large. Strands , the New York Times ' elevated word-search game, requires the player to perform a twist on the classic word search. Words can be made from linked letters up, down, left, right, or diagonal, but words can also change direction, resulting in quirky shapes and patterns. Every single letter in the grid will be part of an answer. There's always a theme linking every solution, along with the "spangram," a special, word or phrase that sums up that day's theme, and spans the entire grid horizontally or vertically. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable By providing an opaque hint and not providing the word list, Strands creates a brain-teasing game that takes a little longer to play than its other games, like Wordle and Connections . title: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 19, 2026
body: Today's Wordle answer should be easy to solve if you like to make food from scratch. If you just want to be told today's word, you can jump to the bottom of this article for today's Wordle solution revealed. But if you'd rather solve it yourself, keep reading for some clues, tips, and strategies to assist you. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 19, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? title: Netflix bought Ben Afflecks AI startup for $587 million
body: Netflix paid over half a billion dollars for Ben Affleck's AI startup InterPositive, which he founded in 2022. According to Variety , Netflix's Form 10-Q report with the Securities and Exchange Commission disclosed that it paid $587 million in cash for an acquisition in March. While the company they acquired goes unnamed in the report, the InterPositive acquisition was announced on March 5, and a Bloomberg report estimated that Netflix could have paid up to $600 million. SEE ALSO: Netflix's 'Wonka' reality show will use AI-generated voice of Gene Wilder According to the March 5 deal announcement , Netflix viewed its acquisition of InterPositive as "investing in creator-led innovation that keeps filmmakers at the center of the process." In that same announcement, Affleck wrote: "In 2022, I spent a lot of time observing the early rise of AI in production. As a filmmaker, I could see how these models came up short. For artists to apply these tools towards telling the stories we dedicate our lives to, they need to be purpose-built to represent and protect all the qualities that make a great story." In a video released alongside the acquisition announcement , Affleck emphasized that InterPositive was "not about text prompting or generating something from nothing." Instead, he explained, InterPositive's tools build a model specific to the film being made, which then gets employed during post-production for processes like mixing and coloring. According to Affleck, this would allow filmmakers to focus on performances in the moment. As reported by Deadline in April , a 2024 patent a

body: The Pixel 10a shipped with a last gen processor. | Photo: Dominic Preston / The Verge Mystic Leaks suggests that the Pixel 11a will return to featuring a flagship-grade processor with the Tensor G6. Rather than the Tensor G5 found in the Pixel 10 and 10 Pro , the Pixel 10a shipped with the previous generation Tensor G4. That was a huge disappointment since, typically, the Pixel a lineup kept the modern processor, but cut corners in other places to keep costs down. The Tensor G6 is rumored to feature the same PowerVR DXT-48-1536 GPU as the G5, but it should still be an improvement over the Mali-G715 in the Tensor G4. title: A live-action Princess Tiana movie is in the works from Colman Domingo and Robert OHara
body: A new Disney princess might be getting the live-action treatment soon: Princess Tiana, from 2009's The Princess and the Frog . SEE ALSO: 'The Odyssey' sails towards a record-breaking opening weekend On Friday, Deadline reported that Emmy and Oscar nominee Colman Domingo ( Euphoria , Sing Sing ) and Tony-nominated director Robert O'Hara ( Slave Play ) are in talks to co-write a live-action film centered on Tiana. Originally voiced by Anika Noni Rose, Tiana is a determined waitress from New Orleans who hopes to one day open her own restaurant. Her grand plan gets derailed when she's turned into a frog alongside Maldonia's cursed Prince Naveen (voiced by Bruce Campos). So begins an adventure through the Louisiana bayou, with the two eventually falling in love. By the end of the film, the pair return to their human forms and get married. That's not all: Tiana's dreams finally come true with the grand opening of her restaurant, Tiana's Palace. However, Domingo and O'Hara's film, still in extremely early days, reportedly won't just be a live-action retread of The Princess and the Frog , the way most of Disney's live-action projects are. Instead, it is set to be an original spin-off story, similar to Beauty and the Beast spin-off Gaston , also in development. The original take on a beloved character could be just what Disney needs as it continues with its live-action projects. 2026's Moana and 2025's Snow White both floundered at the box office, suggesting audiences are growing tired with the constant remakes of classics. However, 2025's Lilo Stitch did earn over $1 billion, so th

The standalone camera includes most of those accessories, but has fewer mounts, a single battery, no SD card, and no extension pole. title: The Odyssey sails towards a record-breaking opening weekend
body: True to its source material, Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is headed for an epic opening weekend at the box office. SEE ALSO: 'The Odyssey' review: Christopher Nolan turns an epic myth into a movie masterpiece According to The Hollywood Reporter , The Odyssey raked in $17.6 million from domestic Thursday night previews and is on track for a $117 million opening weekend. That would make it the best domestic opening for a live-action film this year, surpassing Michael , which took in $97 million and went on to cross the $1 billion mark worldwide in July. The two best domestic openings of the year belong to animated films: Toy Story 5 ($160 million) and The Super Mario Galaxy Movie ($131.7 million). A $117 million opening would also be the best opening for an R-rated film in 2026, overtaking Backrooms ($81.4 million). It would also be the best opening for an R-rated movie from Universal Pictures, a record formerly held by Fifty Shades of Grey ($85.1 million). The Odyssey 's opening weekend box office is also set to surpass that of Nolan's prior film, Oppenheimer ($82.4 million), making it his biggest since The Dark Knight Rises, which still holds a commanding lead with $160 million. Based on The Odyssey 's reported production cost of $250 million and reported marketing cost of $125 million, Forbes estimates that after theaters take their cuts, it will take between $625 to $750 million for The Odyssey to break even. title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
author: Joshua Cohen, Undark Magazine
body: If you’re like me, you or a loved one has struggled through the process of gaining pre-approval for the medical care that your physician has recommended. Personal stories abound regarding the tribulations of patients as they go through hoops to get their health insurer to pay for certain prescription medications, medical procedures, and more. When used judiciously, this process—known as prior authorization —serves as a check on overuse and spending on services or technologies for which there are less costly alternatives. But a large majority of physicians voice concerns about care delays, which can cause patients to abandon recommended treatments while waiting for the insurance company to verify their eligibility and confirm that the treatment is, indeed, medically necessary. Patients who are denied care may submit an appeal, but that requires more time. AI might be able to help. With its ability to efficiently sort through vast reams of information, artificial intelligence could theoretically expedite approval of unambiguously allowable claims, thereby reducing care delays. However, AI-driven prior authorization is facing resistance, as it may increase wrongful denials of health insurance coverage. A 2025 American Medical Association survey of physicians revealed significant concern about the application of AI tools , with 61 percent of doctors worrying that AI will exacerbate denials of what they deem are necessary treatments. title: The best air purifiers for wildfire smoke tested: Our faves from Blueair, Coway Airmega, Shark, and more
body: Can wildfire smoke get into your house? Yes. It won't be visibly hazy like it is outside, but particulate matter like wildfire smoke 20 to 30 times smaller than a human hair can seep into your home in several different ways. Many air purifiers have sensors that provide live air quality metrics from the room they're in, including PM2.5 (particulate matter). Indoor air still isn't innately clean when there's not a major air quality event happening. Indoor air pollution can be caused by cleaning products, cooking smoke, or microplastics embedded into dust or pet dander. It's all the more reason to keep an air purifier running 24/7 at home, regardless of whether you have allergies or not. Just how bad is wildfire smoke for our health? According to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention , wildfire smoke can cause breathing issues, headaches, lethargy, coughing, and wheezing, to name a few ill effects. This applies to everyone after repeated exposure, not just "sensitive groups." Even if you feel fine, smoke particles are small enough to get into our bloodstream. According to Yale Medicine , that means they also have access to other organs like the heart and the liver. Over time, exposure to PM 2.5 might lead to an increased risk of cancers, heart attack, and cognitive issues. Particulate matter like smoke can also mess with your skin barrier and trigger oxidative stress, actively working against your skincare routine. Those who have asthma, heart disease, or diabetes are at an increased risk of getting sick. Kids and pregnant people are also considered high-risk whe
